programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: The Amazing Spider-Man
programme.desc: A lab accident and a medical experiment transform the lives of high-schooler Peter Parker and scientist Dr Curt Connors with unexpected consequences.

programme.title: The Amazing Spider-Man 2
programme.desc: Peter Parker’s future feels uncertain as he struggles to protect New York as Spidey, work out his relationship with Gwen Stacy and deal with his father’s legacy.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are dropped deep in the Bolivian rainforest armed only with three shabby 4x4s, which they bought from the local small ads for a maximum of £3,500 each. What follows is one of their most extraordinary journeys. They attempt to drive from the heart of Bolivia to the coast of Chile, encountering local drug lords, the debilitating effects of high altitude and the terrifying sheer drops of the infamous Death Road along the way. But before all that, they've got to escape from the deep, dark centre of the rainforest.

programme.title: Zara McDermott
programme.desc: With access to luxury clubs, yachts and villas, as well as police, undercover detectives and emergency medics, Zara McDermott discovers the secrets of this unique island.

programme.title: Golf: Scottish Open
programme.desc: Highlights from Sunday’s play as a stacked field featuring the world’s best players compete for the Genesis Scottish Open at The Renaissance Club in East Lothian. Masters Champion Rory McIlroy and World Number One Scottie Scheffler headline a stellar start list for the event which takes place a week before the 154th Open Championship. American Chris Gotterup is in great form as he looks to defend the title he won in 2025, with the home challengers including PGA Champion and 2020 Scottish Open winner Aaron Rai, 2024 winner Bob McIntyre, along with Tommy Fleetwood, Tyrrell Hatton, Marco Penge and the Fitzpatrick brothers.

programme.title: Kevin Bridges: In Search of the Beautiful Game
programme.desc: Kevin Bridges embarks on a journey to see if the beautiful game still exists. He meets Brazil football icon Cafu, Scottish legend John McGinn, Sao Paulo ultras, US football fans and many others.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: An expert witness uses fibre analysis to solve an execution-style murder, and after 26 years in prison, a man walks free when a forensic scientist links DNA to the real killer.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Viewers will be checking their change after watching this episode in Kent! David Harper meets a coin collector who reveals which rare 50 pence piece is worth 300 times its face value! John Wilson’s collection boasts 11,000 coins, including early medieval and Tudor hammered coins and the very first milled ones from the 18th century. Hoping to splash some cash of their own at Detling antiques fair are two teams of bargain hunters. In red are mum and daughter Nisha and dancer Adzi, with their expert Mark Stacey. Their opponents in blue are partners and musicians Kathy and Nardo. Nardo used to play drums in 1980s band Amazulu, and Kathy worked with some big recording artists of the era. They hope to compose a win with expert Kate Bliss, kicking off with a dolphin water feature, but four figures is too much to splash out on. The reds, meanwhile, are pinning their hopes on a set of cameo brooches, but Mark has doubts whether they will prove popular at auction. In Windsor, David hears auctioneer Harry Ballin’s estimates before the teams see their items go under the hammer – for a profit or a loss!

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restore a ceremonial Chinese lion dance costume, a set of well-loved traditional beer pumps and two beautiful ceramic maquettes.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall, Jodie Kidd, Jamali Maddix and Suzi Perry take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quick-fire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games kitchen composter?

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents another semi-final of the classic quiz where contenders return to the black chair under the glare of the spotlight and the ticking clock to win a place in the grand final. The specialist subjects are the Norman Conquest, Borussia Dortmund, popes of the Renaissance and the film musicals of Alan Menken.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Sarah is a passionate livestock farmer and devoted to her Kunekune pigs. As she heads to the south east’s famous Edenbridge and Oxted Agricultural Show, she has high hopes of them walking away as rosette winners. Meanwhile, farming couple Frances and Simon are making their debut appearance at the show with their pedigree Dexter cattle, some of which play an important conservation role grazing in the South Downs.

programme.title: The Hidden World of Hospitality with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Tom explores a part of the industry close to his heart: the highly skilled and competitive world of fine dining. He meets chefs who, like him, are obsessed with the pursuit of culinary excellence. Is it possible to reach perfection when chefs are, by nature, never satisfied?

programme.title: Yellowstone: Wildest Winter to Blazing Summer
programme.desc: Patrick follows the grizzly bears that are taking a risk with the weather by leaving their winter dens early. Hungry wolves are struggling to bring down their elk prey in the unusually shallow snow. And for great grey owls, it is the iciness of the snow that is hampering their hunts. Yellowstone's winter is always one of the most brutal on the planet. But 2016 saw weather records broken, and the wildlife was forced to adapt to survive. Kate Humble gets to grips with the science behind this remarkable season, from understanding the importance of the snowpack's structure as the melt begins to uncovering why Yellowstone's unique geology poses problems for some grazers' teeth.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Chiddingstone Castle in Kent. He visits Anne Boleyn's family home, Hever Castle, and reveals what influenced her rise to power. Antiques experts Claire Rawle and Adam Partridge unearth unique antiques and collectables that reflect the local Kent character.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Eniola Aluko, Phil Daniels, Nikita Kanda and Danny Miller take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games retro bag?

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: If you could do just one thing to transform your health, what should it be? Zoe Ball, Roman Kemp and Clive Myrie show how one small change can make a big difference. From dancing yourself happier to eating mackerel for your heart, to soaking in a hot bath to help you sleep – they share the simple and surprising things we could all do to improve our lives. In postcard-perfect Rye in East Sussex, busy mum Sam is on a mission to supercharge her health after shedding the pounds with the help of weight-loss jabs. Clive Myrie has a simple but powerful idea for her next step – eating oily fish. And it turns out that adding this one food to your diet packs a serious health punch. Omega-3-rich heroes like mackerel, salmon and sardines won’t just help keep the weight off – they could sharpen mental focus, calm inflammation and even lower the risk of heart attacks and strokes.

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: Bringing forensic journalism from all corners of the world.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents the first heat of the classic quiz where contenders face the famous black chair with the ultimate goal of becoming the next Mastermind champion. The specialist subjects are 15th-century king Edward IV, the band Depeche Mode, the films of Yorgos Lanthimos and Roy Keane.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts the quiz where knowledge only gets you so far – as always, the vital extra ingredients are logic and lateral thinking. Three rowers take on a team of microbiologists in finding connections between apparently random clues. For example: Germany, HM Treasury, the Galactic Republic and the Duchy of Lancaster. What connects them?

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The series kicks off with a match between students from Bristol and Imperial. Amol Rajan asks the questions.

programme.title: Evolution
programme.desc: Chris Packham embarks on an extraordinary journey through deep time to uncover how a creature as vast, complex and charismatic as the elephant came to be. From a tiny cell over four billion years ago to one of the planet’s most iconic giants, this is the surprising story of life's most radical transformation.

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob accept a special invitation to fish the estate lake at Burghley House, which sits on the border of Cambridgeshire and Lincolnshire. Built in the 16th century by William Cecil, Elizabeth I’s lord high chancellor, Burghley House is home to some of the most majestic landscapes Paul and Bob have ever found themselves in. With a spectacular lake and stunning gardens, all designed by the iconic Capability Brown, Paul and Bob hope they can do the place justice - and net a decent catch.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

programme.title: Safe Haven
programme.desc: A young woman with a mysterious past lands in Southport, North Carolina, where her bond with a widower forces her to confront the dark secret that haunts her.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani begins a four-part journey along England’s east coast, exploring the wildlife, landscapes and communities adapting to life on the edge of the sea. Her first stop is Northey Island in Essex, an internationally important refuge for threatened birds, including curlew, lapwing and dunlin. With rising sea levels putting increasing pressure on coastal habitats, Anita discovers an innovative conservation project using retired Thames barges to help protect the island’s future. She also meets the volunteers working to safeguard vulnerable ringed plover nests using a simple but effective solution. Anita also delves into the Countryfile archives to revisit some of the projects helping to create more space for nature to thrive.

programme.title: Why...?
programme.desc: During intense storms on 19 August 2024, the 54m superyacht Bayesian mysteriously sinks off the coast of Sicily. Seven people onboard tragically perish. As search and rescue teams scour the coast for survivors, questions begin to be asked. Why did a multi-million-pound superyacht sink when others nearby didn’t? Could its record-breaking mast have made it more vulnerable to strong winds? This film forensically examines what went wrong and asks whether more needs to be done to keep ships safe at sea. Eyewitnesses, first responders, a former captain of the Bayesian and a retired MAIB principal investigator detail what happened.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay returns for the penultimate day of this week’s quiz as the trip heads to Kirkby Lonsdale. With the leaderboard tightening, presenter Owain Wyn Evans, Olympic champion Greg Rutherford and Blue Peter’s Abby Cook are all vying for victory. Paul Gorton resumes his role as unreliable tour guide, scattering facts and fabrications as the competition edges closer to the finale.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Jan’s past is packed with precious memories, but it’s time to cash in on keepsakes for her granddaughter’s future. Can the team turn love into loot when the gavel goes down?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in north east Wales, helping a young couple hoping to find a slice of countryside heaven with room for their two children, a dog, veg patches and some chickens. From an eco-friendly modern home to a hillside mystery house with spectacular views, there’s plenty to explore. Alistair also meets a third-generation farmer who believes healthy soil is the main ingredient for a happy and sustainable future.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.desc: Police in Hertfordshire respond to an emergency that uncovers a shocking crime, Scottish fire crews battle a wildfire visible from space, and Paul Colgan joins farm safety training.

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: In this what-happened-next special, the team search again for a daughter who cares for her mum to try to find the second ring she lost while wild swimming. Archive detective Sam has one more go at cracking the case of a camera filled with memories that so far no-one has claimed. Finally, a few special moments for people from the series after the cameras stopped.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall, Jodie Kidd, Jamali Maddix and Suzi Perry take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quick-fire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games kitchen composter?

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents the third semi-final of the classic quiz where contenders return to the black chair under the glare of the spotlight and the ticking clock to win a place in the grand final. The specialist subjects are the PDC World Darts Championship, the history of transportation to Australia, Parks and Recreation, and Queen Anne.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Derbyshire duo Becky and Matt hold down second jobs to fund their dream of rearing their flock of pedigree Dutch spotted sheep. Winning rosettes at the Royal Cheshire County Show is recognition for breeding the best, so Becky is going all out for a prize even though it’s her first time competing here. Joining them in the show ring with their own Dutch spotted contenders are farming couple Sharron and Peter, who live in Yorkshire. They have been passionate promoters of this breed from the get-go, so a win for them will mean their hard work has been worth it.

programme.title: The Hidden World of Hospitality with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Tom continues his deep dive into the industry he knows and loves. This time, he meets brave and ambitious entrepreneurs looking to expand their businesses in the midst of some of the toughest economic conditions the industry has ever seen. In the high-risk game of hospitality, when do you twist, stick or fold?

programme.title: Yellowstone: Wildest Winter to Blazing Summer
programme.desc: The story of animals surviving one of the harshest seasonal changes on the planet continues. As winter turns to spring, temperatures rise and Yellowstone bursts into life. Beavers feast on new green shoots, baby bison take their first faltering steps and grizzly bear cubs emerge from dens to explore this new green world. But spring is also a perilous time. Wolves are hungrier than usual after slim winter pickings. And rising temperatures melt the vast mountain snow pack, sending a million tons of water flooding into the rivers. Only the toughest will survive.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: This episode revisits some of the locations from the series, with never-seen-before valuations. It's a special show touching down at the Ulster Folk and Transport Museum, Croombe in south Worcestershire and Crowcombe Court in Somerset. We find some fascinating items to take to auction, and Paul Martin discovers a hidden pocket of Cornwall and the historic estate of Mount Edgcumbe.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Eniola Aluko, Phil Daniels, Nikita Kanda and Danny Miller take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games retro bag?

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Sheffield, empty nesters Siva and Dhami are feeling stuck in a rut. But Roman Kemp is about to shake things up. He wants to show them how taking up dancing could do far more than just put a spring in their step. Research reveals that adding dance to your weekly routine can boost fitness, increase brain power and lift your mood. A little rhythm might just rewrite Siva and Dhami's whole routine.

programme.title: Rick Stein’s Food Stories
programme.desc: To end his UK food tour, Rick goes night fishing in Wales and meets the farmer dodging WW2 bombs to raise salt marsh lamb. He visits the spot where Dylan Thomas wrote Under Milk Wood, then heads to Bristol for the ultimate jerk chicken. In the West Country, he meets a pioneer of the organic food movement, a famous female cheesemaker, and chef Mark Hix, who cooks him pollock, cockles and seashore veg.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In the west of the UK, a spectacular monument older than Stonehenge, a 200-year-old mine trapped in time and a lost medieval friary.

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig celebrates the hundredth birthday of the BBC with Zoe Lyons, Greg James, Richard Osman and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: The magnificent Jen Brister introduces comedians Esther Manito and Adam Rowe to the stage.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Paddy O’Connell.

programme.title: ODI Cricket
programme.desc: Highlights from the first ODI of 2026 between England and India.

programme.title: On the Basis of Sex
programme.desc: The story of a young Ruth Bader Ginsburg – then a struggling attorney and new mother – who faces adversity and numerous obstacles in her fight for equal rights throughout her career. When Ruth takes on a groundbreaking tax case with her husband, attorney Martin Ginsburg, she knows it could change the direction of her career and the way the courts view gender discrimination. The film also chronicles the romance between Ruth and Marty, a partnership that succeeded both personally and professionally.

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie’s juggling college and family life, learning about teenage mental health, online pressures and autism - and how care reaches far beyond the classroom.

programme.title: On the Front Line
programme.desc: It started with the seizure of illegal cigarettes hidden in the back room of a minimart in Rochdale and ended with cocaine being bought over the counter from a shop in the West Midlands. Over a year, BBC news investigated the crime happening in plain sight on high streets across the country and, in the process, exposed the crime gangs exploiting the vulnerable and setting up shop in Britain.

programme.title: The Road to Hope with Fergal Keane
programme.desc: The BBC’s Fergal Keane has spent 40 years witnessing the worst that humanity can do - genocide, war, and famine. So why is he full of hope? The answer lies in the extraordinary bravery he’s encountered in the worst places. In this powerful and humane film by Oscar-winner Alice Doyard, Fergal argues that despite the anxiety of the present we have real cause for hope.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Auctioneer James Broad and restorer Saf Fakir travel to Manchester to help Caroline, whose life was turned upside down when her husband Nick was diagnosed with early-onset Alzheimer’s. Now in a care home, Nick’s absence has left Caroline alone in the family home they built together, surrounded by a lifetime of memories. With her sister-in-law Heather by her side, she’s now ready to face the emotional challenge of sorting through Nick's collections. JB uncovers family heirlooms and fascinating finds, from vintage toys and enamel signs to a remarkable collection of sketches by celebrated Manchester artist Harry Rutherford. Meanwhile, Saf works his magic on a well-loved bamboo hall stand and a broken vintage sign that needs some TLC. He’s determined to restore them to their former glory. Every item sold brings Caroline a step closer to easing the cost of Nick’s ongoing care and to finding peace as she begins a new chapter of her own.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Denise Nurse is in the Gloucestershire countryside, helping Charlotte from Croydon in south London find a rural escape with help from her eldest son, Ben. Included in the fantastic mix of properties are character cottages, a spacious new build and an imaginative conversion, offering plenty to tempt her. Taking a break from house hunting, Denise then heads to a local farm to meet some adorable spring lambs before getting stuck into feeding time.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael leaves Kyushu by foot tunnel under the Kanmon straits to arrive on the main island of Honshu at the harbour town of Shimonoseki. Here, he encounters the deadly delicacy of pufferfish. Containing a neurotoxin with no known antidote, only highly trained chefs can prepare the fish. Will Michael give it a try? Travelling eastwards along the Honshu coast, Michael visits Hitachi’s Kasado Works, where the iconic Shinkansen high-speed trains are manufactured. He is thrilled to get inside the factory and see these amazing machines up close. To end this leg, Michael arrives by Shinkansen into Hiroshima, the city devastated by the atomic bomb dropped by US forces on 6th August 1945. Michael crosses the city to visit the Peace Memorial Park, where he meets Keiko Ogura, a bomb survivor who was just eight years old on that fateful day.

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo’s railway adventure through Uzbekistan reaches its finale in the historic city of Bukhara. Amidst the medieval Islamic architecture, Michael goes in search of a centuries-old Jewish community. Stepping inside a 420-year-old synagogue and a family home, he’s introduced to unique customs and traditions, and a fragrant green dish that has made Bukharan Jews famous worldwide. Michael grapples with kurash, an ancient form of upright wrestling once used to select the bravest warriors and now also increasingly popular as a sport for young Uzbek women. Michael’s exploration of Uzbekistan culminates in the vast Kyzylkum Desert, where he experiences the life of nomadic tribes who once roamed its vast sandy plains. His Central Asian journey concludes among the horses and riders who brave all to take part in one of the most dangerous equestrian sports in the world.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall, Jodie Kidd, Jamali Maddix and Suzi Perry take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quick-fire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games kitchen composter?

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents the fourth semi-final of the classic quiz where contenders return to the black chair under the glare of the spotlight and the ticking clock to win a place in the grand final. The specialist subjects are Frank Zappa, the life and works of Siegfried Sassoon, pioneering British and Irish female aviators, and winners of the Eurovision Song Contest.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Wiltshire farmers Rebecca and Luke rear their cattle, sheep and pigs with the help of their four children. Always aiming to farm as sustainably as they can, selling their award-winning meat at Stroud Farmers’ Market in Gloucestershire is a vital source of income. It’s a long day, but Luke hopes his barbecued burgers and sausages will be hot favourites with hungry customers. Meanwhile, their daughter Esme is on the charm offensive, determined to do her family proud and pocket as many cold meat sales as she can. Self-taught fruit farmers Helen and David have been selling at Stroud for over 20 years. They have ploughed all their efforts into growing over 250 rare fruit varieties in their Gloucestershire orchard. They pick over 40 tonnes of apples each year, which they sell fresh and bottled as juice and homemade cider. Both families rely on Stroud’s weekly farmers’ market for their livelihood, and it’s full-on for five hours to secure a successful day’s takings.

programme.title: The Hidden World of Hospitality with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Tom Kerridge continues to lift the lid on the UK’s hospitality industry, this time turning the spotlight on staffing. Despite a UK-wide recruitment crisis, Tom discovers how some businesses are finding clever ways to build and retain strong, effective teams.

programme.title: Yellowstone: Wildest Winter to Blazing Summer
programme.desc: The story of animals surviving one of the harshest seasonal changes on the planet continues. It is summer, and the Yellowstone beavers have a new challenge. Will the young survive as the river dries up and the colony is forced to move home? As food becomes scarce, wolves have a surprising strategy to keep their pups fed and grizzly bears are unexpected visitors on a cowboy ranch. By midsummer, the hot dry conditions create a new danger - deadly wildfires burn out of control and threaten to engulf a family of great grey owls. 2016 was the hottest year on earth since records began, and across Yellowstone scientists reveal the effects of rising temperatures on the animals that live here.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques series. Flog It! comes from Hopetoun House near Edinburgh. Antiques experts Adam Partridge and Caroline Hawley find some fascinating antiques and collectibles to take to auction. Paul Martin discovers more about Hopetoun House by talking to the people who work and live there.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Eniola Aluko, Phil Daniels, Nikita Kanda and Danny Miller take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games retro bag?

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Zoe Ball heads to south London to convince charity worker Latoya that gardening could be her secret wellbeing weapon. She is a total beginner, but the science is firmly on her side. Getting hands on with soil can build strength, bolster the immune system and reduce stress. If Latoya is willing to get her hands dirty, she could see her health blossom.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: After being brutally double-crossed by fellow Traitor Harry Clark, Paul Gorton is taking a second swipe at his rival - this time on the hunt to get one up in the world of antiques. The pair are reunited in the Scottish Highlands, vying to be the winner at auction. Aiding and abetting their efforts in Scotland are antiques experts Natasha Raskin Sharp and Roo Irvine, and they hit the road in a Morgan 4/4. In the shops, Harry stumbles on a very apt mug, while Paul resorts to unusual tactics in a bid to bump up his profits. But will it pay off?

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restore a unique pair of cowboy boots, a piece of plaster holding a loved one's message, a puppet that has lost its limbs and a pioneering film-maker's award.

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the second episode of this two-part series, Simon Reeve travels from the Peloponnese peninsula to the rugged and mountainous north of the country. To learn more about Greece and the Greeks, he meets an extraordinary cast of characters, from a group of rebel monks to conservationists caring for an injured bear cub. Getting behind the picture-postcard image of this beautiful country, he finds out how the Greeks are coming to terms with a seemingly endless crisis.

programme.title: Inside No. 9
programme.desc: Dawn and Mick are hosting their annual Last Night of the Proms party, the perfect antidote to post-Brexit Britain. But not everyone is singing from the same song sheet.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Matt Chorley.

programme.title: The Tech Billionaire Takeover
programme.desc: A new generation of tech billionaires believe their technologies can replace democracy as we know it and upend the global financial system. Matt Shea gets inside access to this new super-elite, visiting the sovereign nations they're building and tracing their growing political connections to the Trump administration. Are these men trying to take over the world?

programme.title: Amityville: An Origin Story
programme.desc: In 1975, when George and Kathy Lutz move into their suburban dream home, a cascade of terrifying, unexplainable events drive them out in just 28 days. Those closest to the terror share their stories.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the final day of this week’s quiz as the action arrives in Bangor. TV presenter Owain Wyn Evans, Olympic champion Greg Rutherford and Blue Peter’s Abby Cook return, and Paul Gorton resumes his role of unreliable tour guide, sharing stories, surprises and suspect claims as the overall winner is finally revealed.

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Tune every day from 7.00am.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: After the Farmer manages to drive his tractor into a mud patch, Shaun, Bitzer and the flock try to rescue it from the quagmire. Before long, however, the stranded vehicle sinks far beyond their reach.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: When an old enemy returns, Colonel K must come out of retirement to save the world from an outbreak of rudeness.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Forest inspectors pay the forest a visit to check its super-happy status. Friendlyface sends the heroes on outlandish quests to guarantee results.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: The CEO buys Jocko’s old amusement arcade and vows to tear it down. However, Boy loves the arcade and challenges her to an epic video game battle with the fate of the arcade at stake!

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: Hacker’s Ruff Guide to the World Cup
programme.desc: To celebrate the World Cup Golden Boot award for the player who scores the most goals, Bionic and Diamond from Gladiators compete in the first (and last ever) Golden Flipper Challenge.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy dismantles the toboggan built by the Lemmings in the log cabin.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings organise a drone race throughout the cabin.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Heat-loving hippies move into Mr Krabs' soup vat!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck is a referee at a football match, and Frog sees how much a footballer is cheered for scoring a goal. He is desperate to score one himself, but can he get past Duck's red card?

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck and Frog are working at a supermarket alongside Toad and Flamingo, who keep ruining Duck and Frog's hard work to make them look bad. Why are they being so mean?

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: An international high-tech organisation run by kids, which investigates all things strange, weird and, most importantly, odd.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Gross Alert! Exploring the weird and wonderful world of medicine. From sneezes and snot to pimples and poo, nothing escapes the Doctors' crazy experiments!

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve travels to new depths as he discovers just what it takes to be an aquatic terror. His quest sees him rocket though the water in spectacular style - just like an Opthalmosaurus. Then he heads to his very own Jurassic park to chomp through chain with the power of Sarcosuchus. Finally, the fear factor gets cranked to the max as Steve dives into the murky unknown in the hunt for the infamous predator x.

programme.title: Horrible Science
programme.desc: Science goes too far in Fartenheimer, music goes too far in Heads, Shoulders, Knees, Toes and All the other Bones in the Body, and there's a Swiftian take on the water cycle.

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: The brothers hold a party to welcome a red panda, two cheetah brothers and some sand lizards to the zoo - but will Cam be able to collect the wriggly lizards?

programme.title: Gym Stars
programme.desc: K’Starr and Chase go head-to-head at the South West Championships.

programme.title: The Football Academy
programme.desc: Can under-15s defender Sol lead his team to victory in The Floodlit Cup? Can Konan bounce back with a little help from family? Spurs legend Jenna Schillaci takes on Eloise and Holly in Skill Showdown.

programme.title: Go Get Arty
programme.desc: The Kwapongs build magical fairy houses, internet star DanTDM shows how to draw a gaming pug, and artist Rong Bao’s inflatable art really pops!

programme.title: Cooking Buddies
programme.desc: Get your dancing shoes ready because Buddy and Conker get a call from Jaylen, a young salsa dancer who doesn’t stop moving! His mum is a professional salsa dancer, and Jaylen has learned all his moves from her, as he loves connecting with their Colombian roots through dancing. But there’s one thing that Jaylen loves even more... eggs! He asks Buddy to teach him an egg-cellent meal that he can make for his family. Buddy knows that if Jaylen loves dancing salsa, then he has to get him making salsa too, so he comes up with a cracking egg tortilla wrap filled with guacamole and zingy salsa. They learn to make their own tortilla dough in minutes, an omelette that sticks to the tortilla in the pan and colourful tomato salsa and creamy guacamole – cracking! Jaylen’s keen to get Buddy learning his kind of salsa too, so Buddy learns the basics of posture and footwork with the help of Jaylen… and some colourful socks! Join Buddy, Conker and Jaylen in this episode of Cooking Buddies that gets salsa onto your plate and into your step. You can get all of Buddy’s recipes, and loads more, at bbc.co.uk/food

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.desc: Nitro pranks Diamond when the guest on a live TV show can’t stop pumping! The other Gladiators have fun behind the scenes, pranking the audience at Sheffield Arena.

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Adam and Joe enter a gaming world and embark on a quest to unearth treasure before attending Sharky’s very chaotic party.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: Animated deep sea adventure series. Captain Barnacles and his intrepid band of explorers roam the oceans in search of adventure and fun. When all the Gups get out of control, Barnacles and Kwazii leap onto the backs of some speedy sailfish to round them up.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Twig steals Cosima's volleyball. A chase leads Big Lizard and Cosima to her den... and also to a happy discovery!

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Nugget’s new fitness device sparks a craze on the berg. Running, skating and jumping, the Piris battle it out to score the most points, with a surprise winner.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck is working in a florist, and Frog is a customer who wants to send flowers to his secret crush. However, his strong fragrance sends Duck into a destructive sneezing frenzy.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Frog is walking in the rain when he spots Duck's beach-themed window display and decides it's the perfect place to relax in the sun for a while.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes go on a quest to get Hoofius’s Toby Jug back from the depths of the Riddling Ruins and are faced with a series of puzzles and traps to solve.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: When Hoofius plays his grandfaun’s old magical panpipes, his friends do exactly what he asks them to do - perfect for leading a quest... or so he thinks!

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds a sceptre that covers anything it touches in gold.

programme.title: Shark Bites
programme.desc: Steve Backshall meets the most ravenous shark in the sea, the tiger shark. Known for eating tin cans and even car number plates, these sharks are in fact perfectly honed hunters.

programme.title: Jamie Johnson FC
programme.desc: It’s party time at the Academy as Chelsea organises a glitzy premiere for the first episode of the Future Hawx documentary - but will the team’s friendships survive the night?

programme.title: Jamie Johnson FC
programme.desc: As tempers flare after the latest revelation, Coach Byrne puts the whole team into detention. Mason contemplates giving up football altogether.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep

Strap yourself in for a sensory overload!

programme.desc: The One Zoo Three brothers are on a mission to compose a zoo musical using the sounds of the animals. Lar gibbons Shany and Raff swing into action and audition to be the perfect percussionists, while a health check with Bombura the loud kookaburra sees him in with a shout for lead vocals. Even playful jaguar cub Inka proves she can add a cracking big crunch to the mix! Meanwhile, Aaron reveals some facts about Darwin the hornbill’s hidden talents, Tyler takes on a breathtaking challenge with an anaconda, and the brothers recall their conservation travels to Uganda to see how rangers are helping protect the African elephant population.

programme.title: Cooking Buddies
programme.desc: Buddy and Conker are joined in their cooking den by Alex, a figure skater who wants a meal that will warm him and his friend up after training on the ice! Alex likes meat, but his friend is vegetarian, so he needs Buddy’s help with something that will keep them both happy... Buddy knows that burgers are a winner as they’re super-flexible, so he creates the ultimate burger for Alex that’s half beef mince and half lentils that his friend can swap for veggie mince and lentils. Using lentils also means you use less meat, which is good for you and the planet – win, win! In return, Alex takes Buddy to train on the ice, teaching him the skills to skate forwards, backwards, stop and build up some speed – and how to style it out when you fall over! It turns out that the key to good form is making lemons with your feet – so there’s more in common with the kitchen than Buddy thought! Join Buddy, Conker and Alex as they glide through the recipe and ice rink in a super-tasty episode of Cooking Buddies! You can get all of Buddy’s recipes, and loads more, at bbc.co.uk/food.
